description: India Book (IB), II, 72. Legal testimony. In Judaeo-Arabic. Location: Fustat. Dating: ca. 1156 CE. Sitt al-Ahl bt. Sayyid al-Kull, the widow of Hiba b. Abū Saʿd who had drowned near Aden (see PER H 161 = PGPID 5445), declares that she has received from Abū l-Surūr Peraḥya b. Tiqva ha-Levi known as Ibn al-Amʿaṭ, a certain sum from the estate of her late husband. Sitt al-Ahl's father Sayyid al-Kull is also known as the druggist Hillel b. Naḥman. (Ḥalfon b. Maḍmūn probably obtained a partial release of the confiscated goods.) (Information from Goitein and Friedman, India Traders)
description_he: ספר הודו II,72: עדות שסית אל-אהל אלמנת היבא בן אבו סעד קיבלה כסף מעיזבון בעלה שטבע ליד עדן
